revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|長谷寺 (長野市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hasedera (Nagano) | native_name = 長谷寺 | image = Hase-dera Temple Nagano City.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hasedera (Nagano) | coordinates = {{coord|36.54863888888889|138.0993611111111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|en|Ekādaśamukha|fr|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観音|km|ឯកាទសមុខ|th|เอกาทศมุข|zh|十一面觀音|qid=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}} | website = {{Official website|https://www.hasedera.net/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hasedera (Nagano)'''|長谷寺}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Buddhist temple|lt=Buddhist temple|ar|معبد بوذي|bh|बौद्ध मंदिर|bn|বৌদ্ধ মন্দির|de|Buddhistischer Tempel|en|Buddhist temple|es|Templo budista|fa|معبد بودایی|fi|Buddhalainen temppeli|fr|Temple bouddhiste|id|Tempat ibadah Buddhisme|it|Tempio buddista|ja|寺院|kn|ಬೌದ್ಧ ದೇವಾಲಯ|ko|사찰|mai|बौद्ध मन्दिर|mr|बौद्ध मंदिर|nl|Boeddhistische tempel|nn|Buddhisttempel|no|Buddhistisk tempel|pt|Templo budista|ro|Templu budist|si|බෞද්ධ පන්සල්|sl|Budistični tempelj|sv|Buddhistiska tempel|tr|Budist tapınağı|ur|بدھ مندر|vi|Chùa|wuu|佛寺|zh_min_nan|Hu̍t-sī|zh_yue|佛寺|zh|佛寺|qid=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province|ja|信濃国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11386164|1=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province}} ** Series ordinal: 13 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039919|ltq=Q135039919|qid=Q135039919|1=Tanishinano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039920|ltq=Q135039920|qid=Q135039920|1=Hiokino Shrine (Q135039920)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Nagano (city)|lt=Nagano|af|Nagano|an|Nagano|ar|ناغانو (ناغانو)|arz|ناجانو|ast|Nagano|azb|ناقانو (شهر)|az|Naqano|ban|Nagano, Nagano|ba|Нагано|be|Нагана|bg|Нагано (град)|bn|নাগানো|br|Nagano|ca|Nagano|ceb|Nagano Shi|ce|Нагано|cs|Nagano|da|Nagano|de|Nagano|devoyage|Nagano|el|Ναγκάνο|en|Nagano (city)|envoyage|Nagano|eo|Nagano|eovoyage|Nagano (urbo)|es|Nagano|et|Nagano|eu|Nagano|fa|ناگانو (شهر)|fi|Nagano|fivoyage|Nagano|fo|Nagano|fr|Nagano|frvoyage|Nagano|fy|Nagano (stêd)|ga|Nagano|gl|Nagano|he|נאגנו|hr|Nagano|hu|Nagano|hy|Նագանո|id|Nagano|idvoyage|Nagano|ie|Nagano|io|Nagano|it|Nagano|ja|長野市|javoyage|長野市|ka|ნაგანო (ქალაქი)|kk|Нагано|ko|나가노시|ky|Нагано|lt|Naganas|lv|Nagano|mdf|Нагана (ошсь)|mn|Нагано|mr|नागानो|mzn|ناگانو (شهر)|nl|Nagano (stad)|nn|Nagano|no|Nagano|os|Нагано|pcd|Nagano|pl|Nagano|plvoyage|Nagano|pt|Nagano (cidade)|ptvoyage|Nagano|qu|Nagano|ro|Nagano|ru|Нагано|runews|Категория:Нагано|sco|Nagano, Nagano|sc|Nagano|sh|Nagano|simple|Nagano (city)|sk|Nagano|sl|Nagano|sr|Нагано|srnews|Категорија:Нагано|sv|Nagano|sw|Nagano, Nagano|szl|Nagano|tg|Нагано|th|นางาโนะ (เมือง)|tl|Lungsod ng Nagano|tr|Nagano|tt|Нагано|uk|Нагано|ur|ناگانو، ناگانو|uz|Nagano|vi|Nagano (thành phố)|war|Nagano (syudad)|wuu|长野市|zh_classical|長野市|zh_min_nan|Nagano-chhī|zh_yue|長野市|zh|長野市|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} * {{ill|Q289250|ltq=Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Sarashina District, Nagano|lt=Sarashina district|en|Sarashina District, Nagano|ja|更級郡|qid=Q7423207|1=Sarashina District, Nagano}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.54863888888889|138.0993611111111}} == official website (P856) == * https://www.hasedera.net/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Hase-dera Temple Nagano City.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11404731|ltq=Q11404731|qid=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181981 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == religion or worldview (P140) == * {{ill|Q674374|ltq=Q674374|qid=Q674374|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7100005000739 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Nakatanino Shrine Kamisha|lt=Nakatanino Shrine Kamisha|qid=Q135194158|1=Nakatanino Shrine Kamisha}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+1', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135194159|ltq=Q135194159|qid=Q135194159|1=Nakatanino Shrine Shimosha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市篠ノ井塩崎878番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:長谷寺 (長野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11653682}} == Japanese content == {{日本の寺院 | 名称 = Hasedera (Shinano) | 画像 = [[File:Hase-dera Temple Nagano City.jpg|280px|Photo of temple]]
Kannon Hall | 所在地 = 878 Shionosaki, Shinonoi, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture | 緯度度 = 36| 緯度分 = 32| 緯度秒 = 55.1 | 経度度 = 138| 経度分 = 05| 経度秒 = 57.7 | 山号 = Kinpōzan | 院号 = Ryūfukuin | 宗派 = {{ill|lt=Shingon Buddhism|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}{{ill|lt=Chisan school|Q674374|qid=Q674374|lt=Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} | 本尊 = {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Avalokitesvara|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (Nikuge Kannon) | 創建年 = tradition: {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|qid=Q210688|1=Asuka period}} ({{ill|lt=637|Q33294|qid=Q33294|1=637}}) | 開基 = Hakujō-ō | 正式名 = Kinpōzan Ryūfukuin Hasedera | 別称 = Shinano Kannon Temple
Hase Kannon | 札所等 = No.18 of the {{ill|lt=Shinano 33 Kannon pilgrimage|Q11386159|qid=Q11386159|1=33 sites of Kannon pilgrimage in Shinano}} | 文化財 = | 公式HP = http://www.hasedera.net/ | 公式HP名 = Kinpōzan Hasedera official homepage | 地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Hasedera|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Hasedera''' (はせでら) is a {{ill|lt=temple|Q5393308|qid=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Chisan school|Q674374|qid=Q674374|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} of {{ill|lt=Shingon Buddhism|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} in Shinonoi Shionosaki, {{ill|lt=Nagano|Q128849|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Its mountain name is Kinpōzan. It is commonly called '''Shinano Hasedera'''. Among the many Hasedera temples across Japan, it is one of the three called the "Three Great Hasedera of Japan" (Yamato, Kamakura, and Shinano), together with the {{ill|lt=Hasedera (disambiguation)|Q1588189|qid=Q1588189|lt=Hasedera|1=Hase-dera (disambiguation)}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|lt=Kamakura|Q200267|qid=Q200267|1=Kamakura}}. === History === Temple tradition states that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|qid=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} (629-641), Hakujō (also called Haku-suke), a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q349258|qid=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}}, who had been exiled to this region in his grandfather's generation and settled here, received an oracle from {{ill|lt=Amitabha|Q236242|qid=Q236242|1=Amitābha}} of {{ill|lt=Zenko-ji|Q189640|qid=Q189640|1=Zenkō-ji}} in Shinano and invited an Eleven-faced Kannon from Hatsuseyama in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, which became the origin of this temple. (The exact year is unknown; even the temple website lists only the Asuka period.) The {{ill|lt=Hasedera|Q1457787|qid=Q1457787|1=Hase-dera}} at Hatsuse in Yamato (Sakurai, Nara Prefecture) is said to have begun when Dōmyō of {{ill|lt=Kawahara-dera|Q3194342|qid=Q3194342|1=Kawara-dera}} founded an original Hasedera in {{ill|lt=686|Q30619|qid=Q30619|1=686}} (Shuchō 1), and in {{ill|lt=727|Q23557|qid=Q23557|1=AD 727}} (Jinki 4) Tokudō carved an Eleven-faced Kannon and opened the current Hasedera. While the Yamato Hasedera certainly existed from the Nara period, this foundation story is traditional lore; therefore, how far the origin tradition of Shinano Hasedera reflects historical fact is uncertain. From the mountain behind the temple, a bronze {{ill|lt=sutra container|Q11606503|qid=Q11606503|1=kyōzutsu}} inscribed {{ill|lt=1151|Q19642|qid=Q19642|1=1151}} (Ninpei 1) has been excavated, showing that in that year a monk titled {{ill|lt=Daishi|Q10935264|qid=Q10935264|1=Daishi}} buried copies of the ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|qid=Q861212|lt=Myōhō Rengekyō|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=Muryōjukyō|Q6029801|qid=Q6029801|1=The Amitāyus Sutra}}'' on Mt. Hase. * {{ill|lt=1181|Q19704|qid=Q19704|1=1181}} ({{ill|lt=Yōwa|Q729609|qid=Q729609|1=Yōwa}} 1): In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', guardian Hase Shrine became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. : Around this time, {{ill|lt=Kakumyō|Q11630332|qid=Q11630332|1=Kakumyo Tayubo}} (formerly military strategist of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Q1139202|qid=Q1139202|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, disciple of {{ill|lt=Honen|Q507980|qid=Q507980|1=Hōnen}}, and companion of {{ill|lt=Shinran|Q512934|qid=Q512934|1=Shinran}}) founded Kōraku-ji near Hasedera. * Around {{ill|lt=1200|Q5224|qid=Q5224|1=1200}} ({{ill|lt=Shoji|Q1199890|qid=Q1199890|1=Shōji (era)}} 2), "Hasedera Reigenki" was compiled. * {{ill|lt=1290|Q5552|qid=Q5552|1=1290}} ({{ill|lt=Shoō|Q1327371|qid=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} 3): By a revelatory dream of {{ill|lt=Prince Hisaaki|Q715826|qid=Q715826|1=Prince Hisaaki}}, regent {{ill|lt=Hojo Sadatoki|Q3136681|qid=Q3136681|1=Hōjō Sadatoki}} had monk Shinkai restore Hasedera. * {{ill|lt=1346|Q6080|qid=Q6080|1=1346}} (Southern court: Shohei 1, Northern court: Jōwa 2): tax-exempt fields of Hasedera are recorded in "Tenryu-ji documents." : Around this time, "Sangoku Denki" was compiled and includes the Hakujō legend (Vol. 5, No. 30 "Shinshu Sarashina-gun Hakukeio no Koto"). * {{ill|lt=1400|Q6108|qid=Q6108|1=1400}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 7): warfare occurred around Hasedera due to the {{ill|lt=Battle of Otō|Q11433918|qid=Q11433918|1=Battle of Ōtō}}. * Early Edo period: 15 koku of temple lands were donated by the Tokugawa shogunate. * {{ill|lt=1701|Q6814|qid=Q6814|1=1701}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 14): Koyasu Hall constructed. * {{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|qid=Q6873|1=1713}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3): Kannon Hall rebuilt. * {{ill|lt=1776|Q7703|qid=Q7703|1=1776}} ({{ill|lt=An'ei (era)|Q1193910|qid=Q1193910|lt=An'ei|1=An'ei}} 5): Niō gate constructed. * {{ill|lt=1802|Q6883|qid=Q6883|1=1802}} ({{ill|lt=Kyowa|Q960763|qid=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 2): bell-tower gate constructed. * {{ill|lt=1805|Q6898|qid=Q6898|1=1805}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 2): Jūō Hall constructed; stone foundation collapsed in the Zenkoji earthquake. * {{ill|lt=1847|Q7644|qid=Q7644|1=1847}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|qid=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 4): temple buildings damaged in the {{ill|lt=Zenkoji earthquake|Q11418893|qid=Q11418893|lt=Great Zenkoji earthquake|1=1847 Zenkoji earthquake}}. * {{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|qid=Q7701|1=1863}} ({{ill|lt=Bunkyu|Q730266|qid=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} 3): kuri (priests' quarters) constructed. * {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|qid=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keio|Q776775|qid=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2): founder hall constructed. * Showa period: temple buildings repaired; sutra container excavated. * Heisei period: Kannon Hall, main hall, and kuri renovated; sutra repository built. === Buildings === * Hōjō Hall (main hall) * Kannon Hall * Jūō Hall * Ihai Hall * Fudō Hall * Koyasu Jizō Hall * Niō Gate * Bell-tower gate * Founder Hall * 33 Kannon stone Buddhas * Hase Shrine Upper Shrine * Tomb of Hakujō-ō * Sutra repository * Kuri === Cultural properties === === Nagano Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Nagano Prefectural Treasure (tangible cultural property) ** Wooden standing statue of Jizō Bosatsu - designated March 16, 2017 (Heisei 29){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kokai/kensei/kenpo/h29/h2903/documents/kokuji20170316.pdf Nagano Prefectural Gazette, March 16, 2017]}} per Nagano Prefectural Board of Education Notice No.1 (link on Nagano Prefecture website). === Events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}-{{ill|lt=January 5|Q2202|qid=Q2202|1=January 5}} - New Year and first-goma austerities * {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|qid=Q2228|1=January 7}} - Nanakusa protective seal distribution * {{ill|lt=January 18|Q2265|qid=Q2265|1=January 18}} - First Kannon exorcism prayer meeting * {{ill|lt=March 15|Q2403|qid=Q2403|1=March 15}} - Nirvana assembly and picture explanation * {{ill|lt=March 21|Q2433|qid=Q2433|1=March 21}} - Spring equinox assembly and official portrait offering * {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|qid=Q2521|1=April 18}} - Spring grand festival and great Prajna sutra reading * {{ill|lt=August 9|Q2782|qid=Q2782|1=August 9}} - Ugusai 33 Lanterns (Sanjosa) * {{ill|lt=August 10|Q2779|qid=Q2779|1=August 10}} - Segaki service * {{ill|lt=September 23|Q2888|qid=Q2888|1=September 23}} - Autumn equinox assembly * {{ill|lt=December 12|Q2369|qid=Q2369|1=December 12}} - Hōon-kō * {{ill|lt=December 18|Q2438|qid=Q2438|1=December 18}} - Year-end Kannon rite * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}} - New Year's Eve service and two-year visit * 18th of each month - day associated with Eleven-faced Kannon * Second Sunday each month - sutra-copying gathering === Other events === * Shinano pilgrimage association - regularly conducts pilgrimages to Kannon sacred sites such as Saigoku, Bandō, Chichibu, and Shinano, as well as the {{ill|lt=Shikoku 88-temple pilgrimage|Q1972399|qid=Q1972399|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. * O-ekitoki (picture sermon) - explanation of the Śākyamuni nirvana painting. In addition to March 15, it is also given at this temple and as traveling lectures regardless of sect. === Address === 878 Shionosaki, Shinonoi, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture (Temple booklet lists: Hase Hakujō, Shinonoi Shionosaki, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture) === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Inariyama Station|Q981519|qid=Q981519|1=Inariyama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Shinonoi Line|Q843201|qid=Q843201|1=Shinonoi Line}} ({{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === {{脚注の不足|date=2020年3月|section=1}} * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'', 1981, {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|qid=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} * ''Exploring Ancient Temples of Shinshu: Tendai and Shingon'', 1996, {{ill|lt=Kyodo Shuppansha|Q11643407|qid=Q11643407|1=Kyōdo Publishing}} === External links === * {{Official website|name=Kinpōzan Hasedera Official Homepage}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hasedera (Nagano)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hasedera (Nagano)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hasedera (Nagano)]][[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Temples of Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] [[Category:Shinano 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:長野市の寺]]
